key_insight: For RLVR fine-tuning, orthonormal low-rank initialization gives the closest behavior to full fine-tuning and avoids the instability seen with other structured LoRA initializations.
engineering_relevance: This suggests that initialization geometry can materially affect RLVR stability and performance, so LoRA variants should be chosen differently for reinforcement-based training than for standard SFT.
method: Analyze LoRA behavior under RLVR and compare the gap to full fine-tuning.
Propose geometry-preserving orthonormal initialization, yielding RLPO and RLMO variants.
Explain why PiSSA and MiLoRA can underperform in RLVR despite strong SFT results.
evidence_signal: On mathematical reasoning benchmarks, the proposed orthonormal initialization stabilizes RLVR training and outperforms standard LoRA, while PiSSA and MiLoRA can underperform and become unstable.
watch_item: The benefit is shown on mathematical reasoning RLVR benchmarks, so transfer to other RLVR tasks or model scales may require re-validation.
paper_title: Geometry-Preserving Orthonormal Initialization for Low-Rank Adaptation in RLVR
